summary: Muddy dome recording captures a solid but unremarkable set with Jerry’s decent playing and a few bright moments like Terrapin Station and the Mexicali‑>Estimated Prophet transition.
review: The audience tape reflects the notoriously poor acoustics of the Metrodome – echoing drums, muddy mids and a washy sound that varies from barely tolerable to passably clear on the floor. Musically the show is described as solid but not spectacular; Jerry’s guitar work earns occasional praise while the setlist quirks (Dylan/Petty opening, Terrapin Station, Mexicali Blues → Estimated Prophet) are noted as the only highlights.

ai_rating.rationale: The vast majority of reviews criticize the sound quality and call the performance merely solid, with only a few positive comments about Jerry’s playing and specific song transitions; this yields an overall rating just below average.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Severe echo and reverberation in the dome, muddy mids, wash effect from mis‑aimed sound towers, occasional clarity only for floor listeners; overall a very harsh and uneven listening experience.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Single‑set show with an unusual Dylan/Petty opening followed by the Dead; set feels disjointed but includes a few interesting transitions.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Several reviewers note Jerry’s playing was decent or even mesmerizing despite the bad sound.
band_member_comments.Phil: Comments that Lesh’s bass gets lost in the mix and a anecdote of Phil standing on c‑scaffolding shouting ‘Watch the band!’
band_member_comments.Drums: Frequent remarks about echoing, muddy drum sound and a general lack of clarity for the rhythm section.
